Name: curvature_aware_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Training-Free Representation Geometry
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that a curvature-aware precision routing framework can improve the efficiency and accuracy of training-free representation geometry in machine learning by adaptively allocating precision to the most critical components of the model, based on their curvature and sensitivity to perturbations.
Related Work: While there have been studies on precision routing and training-free representation geometry, our proposal is novel in its focus on curvature-awareness and its integration with linear algebra and spectral quantization theory. Our approach differs from existing work in its emphasis on mechanistic transparency, deterministic numerical guardrails, and rigorous mathematical accounting.
Abstract: This project proposes a curvature-aware precision routing framework for training-free representation geometry in machine learning. Our approach combines spectral quantization theory with low-overhead implementation, bridging foundational optimization theory with deployment constraints. We prioritize mathematical transparency, explicit numerical stability analysis, and strict computational overhead bounds, establishing a highly feasible pipeline for exploratory single-GPU validation. Our framework integrates projection-based mechanistic probes to formally isolate algorithmic pathologies from infrastructural noise, quantifying gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement.
Experiments: Implement the curvature-aware precision routing framework on a single GPU
Evaluate the framework on a range of benchmark datasets and models, using metrics such as accuracy, efficiency, and robustness
Compare the results to existing precision routing approaches, using ablation studies and systematic variance quantification
Investigate the impact of curvature-awareness on the stability and generalization of the model, using techniques such as PAC-Bayes verification and implicit regularization scaling laws
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of the curvature-aware precision routing framework may lead to increased computational overhead
The framework may require significant hyperparameter tuning and calibration
The approach may not generalize well to all types of models and datasets
